title: search — subscribed events
description: Subscribes to core lifecycle events + tenants / user erasure + subscription plan changes for retention tier reconciliation. Scout's own model observers (registered by HasSearchable) fire directly against consumer models — they're not listed here (they attach at the trait, not at the module event bus).

listeners.purpose: Hydrate the SearchIndex registry from #[Searchable] discovery via olvlvl/composer-attribute-collector. Reconciles any drift between the compiled attributes and the search_indexes table (config_hash mismatch = mark index as needs-reindex; missing row = insert with status='reindexing'). Cached under `search:registry` — see caches.json.

Cascade-delete every search_indexes / search_sync_jobs / search_synonyms / search_saved_queries / search_analytics_events row for the tenant via FK cascade. Then dispatch PurgeIndexModelJob(force=true, tenantId=...) to drop physical engine indexes without the 7d swap grace.

GDPR Art. 17 — (1) NULL search_analytics_events.user_id where user_id = erased. (2) SearchSavedQuery: shared queries (is_shared=true) transfer ownership to a tenant admin picked via user::UserAdminPicker; private queries soft-deleted. (3) search_sync_jobs.causer_id anonymised to `erased:{sha256}` — row retained as operational evidence.

Bulk UPDATE search_sync_jobs.retention_tier + search_analytics_events.retention_tier for the tenant based on the new plan's `search.analytics_retention_tier` entitlement. Rows now eligible for longer retention are promoted; rows eligible for shorter are not demoted below their current value.
Same listener, downgrade branch. New rows classified under the lower tier; existing rows keep the higher tier until natural expiry.

Cheap opportunistic check: any of this user's search_sync_jobs stuck in `running` with last_progress_at older than the stale threshold get transitioned to cancelled. Better UX than waiting for the 15-minute scheduled sweep.

When a transfer export job was initiated with `search_reindex_target` in its params (indicating it was generated as source for a search reindex), auto-dispatch the ReindexModelBatchJob with source=xfer_artifact + the artifact id. Enables the export → reindex chain for cross-region migrations without manual intervention.
